checks.a_analysis: The output accurately reflects the headings, lists, and table content of the source document. The titles and summaries for the table are acceptable AI-generated additions for accessibility. No fabricated text or sections were identified.
All URLs present in the JSON (e.g., NC OneMap metadata link) match the source document exactly. No fabricated phone numbers or URLs were introduced.
All primary sections (Welcome, Framework, Working Groups, Regular Status Updates, Other Business, Action Item Review, Adjourn) and the single page are accounted for in the output.
The '2021 Meetings' table contains all 4 rows (header + 3 date rows) found in the source document.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The content within each section is well-preserved. While a footer line is missing, it represents less than 5% of the total document text and does not constitute a major omission of a section's content.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text at the very bottom of the source document containing the committee's primary web links is entirely omitted from the output. This is a wording change (omission) that results in a loss of information found in the original document.
checks.b_evidence_source: SMAC Web Site – http://it.nc.gov/gicc | NC OneMap Web Site - www.nconemap.gov
checks.b_evidence_output: [The text is missing from the end of the JSON sections array]

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the output against the source document. No content from the source PDF appears more times in the output than in the original. The list of meeting dates and the agenda items are present exactly once.

The heading hierarchy is slightly flattened but logically consistent with the source's visual style. The document title and 'Agenda' are H2. Subsection titles are handled via the schema's subsection property. However, 'Action Item Review' and 'Adjourn' are rendered as H3 headings nested within the 'Other Business' subsection. Since 'Action Item Review' and 'Adjourn' are top-level agenda items of equal rank to 'Other Business' in the source, nesting them as H3 subsections of 'Other Business' is a hierarchy error. It does not skip levels (H2 to H3), but the structural assignment is incorrect.
checks.b_evidence_location: Inside the 'Other Business' subsection (index 5 of the subsections array)

checks.a_analysis: The floating '2021 Meetings' block on the right side of the PDF is correctly identified as a table. It includes the header row and three data rows matching the source. The structure (headers, rows) is preserved correctly.
checks.b_evidence_output: The 'table' element within the 'Working Groups' subsection containing 1 header row and 3 data rows.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order generally follows the vertical flow of the document. However, 'Action Item Review' and 'Adjourn' are structurally nested inside the 'Other Business' subsection instead of being standalone sections at the end of the document. In the source, these are distinct agenda items below 'Other Business'.
checks.b_evidence_source: The items 'Action Item Review (5 min.)' and 'Adjourn' appear after the 'Other Business' section.
checks.b_evidence_output: The items are included in the 'elements' array of the 'Other Business' subsection.

checks.a_analysis: The complex nested lists in the source (e.g., NC Board on Geographic Names with its three levels) are correctly captured in the JSON structure with nested 'items' and 'nested_list' properties. The relationship between items and their sub-bullets is maintained.
checks.b_evidence_source:
• NC Board on Geographic Names - Cam McNutt
    o SMAC Discussion and Approval
        ▪ Simone Mountain
        ▪ Stiles Mountain
checks.b_evidence_output: nested_list structure for 'NC Board on Geographic Names' containing 'SMAC Discussion...' which itself contains another nested_list with 'Simone Mountain' and 'Stiles Mountain'.

checks.a_analysis: The output identifies the 'Join meeting' link and the OneMap URL within the text. However, the contact links/URLs provided in the footer of the document ('http://it.nc.gov/gicc' and 'www.nconemap.gov') are entirely missing from the output JSON.
checks.b_evidence_source: SMAC Web Site – http://it.nc.gov/gicc | NC OneMap Web Site - www.nconemap.gov
checks.b_evidence_output: (Text and links are absent from the JSON elements)

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully reproduces the logical organization and visual flow of the source PDF. The document's structure, from the header information through the various agenda sections (Welcome, Framework, Working Groups, etc.), is maintained in a single-column layout appropriate for web viewing. The '2021 Meetings' block, which is floating to the right in the PDF, has been logically integrated as a table within the main flow, which is a standard responsive layout adjustment. Added elements like the Table of Contents on the left are acceptable accessibility enhancements.

The meeting schedule, which appears as a right-aligned text block in the PDF, has been converted into a structured HTML table with a clear '2021 Meetings Schedule' header. The table is clean, legible, and correctly represents the data (date and meeting names) found in the source. No other tables are present in the document.
The source PDF is a purely text-based agenda and does not contain any logos, charts, diagrams, or other meaningful images. Consequently, no images are missing from the HTML output.

A review of the HTML rendering shows no instances of duplicated body content. While headings appear both in the Table of Contents and the body, this is an expected design pattern for accessible documents and does not constitute a defect.
